title: ProtocolLab TLS Performance Contracts
purpose: Define implementation-neutral TLS 1.3 handshake performance semantics with a minimal validity gate.
scope: The first executable contract slice is a fresh authenticated TLS 1.3 handshake with no resumption, early data, or application data. Resumption, 0-RTT, record throughput, and TLS 1.2 remain later work.

requirements.title: Exact TLS version
requirements.statement: The TLS full-handshake scenario MUST request and observe TLS 1.3 without silent downgrade.

requirements.title: Fresh authenticated handshake
requirements.statement: The TLS full-handshake scenario MUST establish a fresh authenticated session without offered or accepted resumption state and without early data.

requirements.title: Handshake-only workload
requirements.statement: The first TLS handshake workload MUST transfer zero application-data bytes during each measured operation.

requirements.title: Deterministic certificate identity
requirements.statement: The TLS full-handshake scenario MUST bind SNI, ALPN, certificate-profile identity, leaf DER fingerprint, leaf SPKI fingerprint, chain shape, and trust-anchor identity to `plab-single-leaf-p256-v1`.

requirements.title: TLS handshake validity gate
requirements.statement: TLS handshake measurements MUST require the selected executor and generator, exact TLS 1.3, authenticated peer, fresh session, expected ALPN and certificate profile, successful completion, and zero unexpected failures or timeouts.

requirements.title: TLS-only latency boundary
requirements.statement: The primary TLS handshake latency MUST begin when client TLS handshake processing starts on an established TCP connection and end when authenticated TLS 1.3 establishment completes.

requirements.title: Connection-inclusive diagnostic latency
requirements.statement: TLS handshake evidence MAY report a separate diagnostic latency from TCP-connect initiation through authenticated TLS completion.

requirements.title: TLS metrics and artifacts
requirements.statement: The TLS full-handshake scenario MUST declare handshake rate, TLS-only latency mean and percentiles, completed, failed and timed-out counts, negotiated cryptographic metadata, and raw negotiation, topology, validation, stdout, stderr, and normalized-result artifacts.
